topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: Most likely best WR on board at 1.06 after Tate/Lemon likely gone (ADP 9.46/11.18). RSP DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp aligns with both RSP and consensus boards — exactly the type of pick a medium-RSP-affinity GM blends to.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Likely available at 1.06 if Fields is gone. RSP DoT 86.3 Starter grade and ADP 13.57 give consensus-plus-RSP overlap. Versatile flanker/slot fits a WR room that already has 7 bodies but lacks a clear young alpha.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Premier 1.17 target — only 2 TEs on the roster makes TE the clearest positional need. RSP DoT 84.4 Rotational Starter on cusp of Starter tier with classic move-TE profile, ADP 21.25 means he'll likely still be there at 1.17.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: If he slides to 1.06 (ADP 12.71) the value is too good to pass — boom-bust profile but RSP Rotational Starter at 84.5 DoT. Fits a $2.9M cap squeeze better as a taxi stash than a high-floor active piece.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Round 2 (2.06) target despite having 6 RBs — RSP DoT 84.1 with elite recruiting pedigree, ADP 25.83 means he'll be available. With 4 contracts expiring this year, RB depth turnover is plausible and Singleton offers second/third-round value per RSP.

capPosture: Tight at $2.9M cap space against $45M cap — 1.06 rookie minimum salary likely forces a taxi designation (50% hit) or shedding an expiring veteran before activation.

wildcard.name: Kenyon Sadiq
wildcard.reasoning: If Stowers is gone by 1.17, Sadiq becomes the TE pivot — RSP DoT 82.3 with Brock Bowers high-end comp and elite Combine numbers (ADP 13.11 suggests he could be a reach at 1.17 but a steal at 2.06 if he falls). Doubles down on the TE-is-thin reality.

summary: Running down the Dream enters with balanced positional counts but a clear TE deficit (only 2) and a $2.9M cap crunch that forces creative roster construction. Expect a best-WR-available swing at 1.06 (Fields or Concepcion), a deliberate TE grab at 1.17 (Stowers preferred, Sadiq as fallback), and developmental upside picks in rounds 2-3 that can sit on taxi at half-price while expiring contracts roll off.
